author: m.kalinin
content: Is it worth an increased client complexity to send ptc votes on non-canonical branches? Honest proposer will keep building on full blocks so the liveness of a side chain with lack of PTC votes won't degrade completely, what we seem to loose is builder protection which looks fine given the network conditions that have led to a fork starting beyond `dependent_root`

author: potuz
content:
The way I see it is that we need the protocol to be rational for all players when possible and specially when there's money involved. Without this, it's rational for the proposer to reorg payloads. It also seems to me to be a good rework that I wanted to do in the VC anyway: I believe that's the right design for duties: beacon driven instead of vc driven: the VC calls the beacon to register some keys and then regularly calls the beacon with "do I have anything to do now?" 

But yes, I believe we should not be touching this now. At least we aren't changing this at all for a while

author: 0xunclebill
content: I feel like we should at least make the api change sooner than later though. If not we’d have to introduce a v2 endpoint if we don’t get to this in time for the Gloas fork. Having to maintain the current + new PTC duty flows would be pretty bad imo

author: m.kalinin
content:
> The way I see it is that we need the protocol to be rational for all players when possible and specially when there's money involved.

I believe there can be other concerns in the case when you have to ptc-vote on a different branch. I haven't done a rigor analysis but i believe a competing branch of two-epochs length cannot emerge in a healthy network. It indicates either a presence of a large adversary or asynchrony or the the mix of the two. In general, are ePBS guarantees and properties hold under asynchrony and large adversary? If not, it might not worth preserving that bit of security as there is already a breach in another place

author: potuz
content: These forks exist on every single devnet when there's a bug. I'm not that concerned with attackers cause any attacker that can cause such a long running fork without bugs can do much more damage other ways

author: m.kalinin
content: there must be participation rate drop in these situations which weakens other guarantees including LMD-GHOST, and if that other branch attracting a non-negligible support i would suppose the drop should be significant. i am not saying honest vals should not ptc-vote on competing branches, trying to evaluate the comlexity of this from the protocol perspective

author: potuz
content: The protocol complexity is zero here afaict

author: m.kalinin
content: yes, the engineering and data complexity could be an issue as the competing branch can be quite long and you'd have to compute shuffling all the time you see a block from that branch
